mnemonics: Regular Chains Always Settle Down (RCASD): Regular Markov Chains will always have a unique Stationary Distribution.
Absorbing States Suck Everything In (ASSEI): Once you enter an absorbing state, you can never leave.
keyPoints: A stationary distribution π satisfies π = πP, meaning it remains unchanged after a transition.
A Markov chain is regular if some power of its transition matrix P has all positive entries (no zeros).
Absorbing states are states with a probability of 1 to remain in themselves (a '1' on the diagonal of P).
commonMistakes: Assuming all Markov chains have a stationary distribution; only regular and some other types do.
Confusing stationary distribution (long-run behavior) with limiting distribution (the limit of P^n).
Thinking you can escape an absorbing state; by definition, the probability of leaving is zero.
